title: CHARACTERS
text: CHARACTERS Operation activated by the SAX parser when it encounters character data after the start of an element. Explain this code operation CHARACTERS params string Chars: in endparams Implement your code here end; CHARACTERS operation Copy Parameters Chars —character data to a maximum of 10240000 bytes. Description SAX parsers for Java and C++ call their character() method multiple times with little chunks of the data, making it the programmers responsibility to concatenate these chunks into one string. The UXMLREADER invokes CHARACTERS only once, unless the character data exceeds 10240000 bytes. In this case, CHARACTERS is called multiple times with chunks that fit inside 10240000 byte limit. XML entities, such as & are replaced in the data by the actual character they represent. The same is true for character data defined with the <![CDATA[…]]> XML instruction—you will not find <!CDATA… in the Chars parameter. Character data can include white space. If there is a DTD for the XML document and it contains whitespace that the parse considers ignorable, the parser activates the WHITESPACE operation, if present. Related Links WHITESPACE Operation activated by the SAX parser when it encounters character data that it considers to be ignorable whitespace. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
